hawkish_phrases: it would likely soon be appropriate for the Committee to take another step in removing policy accommodation
continuing to raise the target range for the federal funds rate gradually would likely be appropriate
inflation on a 12-month basis would likely move slightly above the Committee's 2 percent objective for a time
the federal funds rate could be at or above their estimates of its longer-run normal level before too long
the Committee expects that economic conditions will evolve in a manner that will warrant further gradual increases in the federal funds rate
dovish_phrases: it was premature to conclude that inflation would remain at levels around 2 percent

adjustments in the stance of policy should take account of the possibility that longer-term inflation expectations have drifted a bit below levels consistent with the Committee's 2 percent inflation objective
the stance of monetary policy remains accommodative
the federal funds rate is likely to remain, for some time, below levels that are expected to prevail in the longer run
Market-based measures of inflation compensation remain low
summary: The FOMC voted unanimously to hold rates at 1-1/2 to 1-3/4 percent with explicit forward guidance that further gradual increases would likely soon be appropriate, reflecting confidence that inflation had moved close to 2 percent and economic activity would continue expanding at a moderate pace.
